page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, offering 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz and includes 35 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and is optimized for both network and EBS performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. In benchmark evaluations, the m5dn.4xlarge demonstrates average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, though its single-core performance in stress-ng and compression tasks falls into the lower tier. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression and Geekbench compilation, consistently register in the middle tier, alongside a Redis SET throughput of 13.2 million operations per second.

From a resource-density perspective, the inclusion of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage and enhanced networking capabilities provides a balanced hardware profile for data-intensive applications without requiring specialized storage-only instances. The instance is suited for workloads such as relational databases, caching layers, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications that benefit from local scratch space and consistent network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average multi-core processing capabilities, average memory bandwidth, and steady database operations, though single-core execution speeds are relatively modest. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for balanced workloads such as relational databases, caching layers, and static web serving that require local high-speed storage and reliable network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, 600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.4xlarge features 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 600 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-speed local NVMe storage and optimized network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

600 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage
